sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 1-week history of low back pain that began after twisting and possibly lifting something on the left side, which initially resolved but returned after a few hours.
The pain is located in the middle to lower back, with exacerbation when twisting suddenly to the left.
The patient reports that the pain is worse when pressing down on the low back, particularly in the center.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and is on metformin twice a day.
The patient has a family history of owning a bakery, and occasionally visits the bakery but tries to control their intake of sweets.
Review of systems: The patient reports occasional trouble with the left eye, but this is not related to the low back pain.
Current medications: Metformin 2x/day.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear, heart rate and rhythm are regular, and there is no pain when pressing down on the upper back.
The patient experiences pain when pressing down on the low back, particularly in the center.
The patient reports pain when lifting the right and left legs.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has a musculoskeletal low back strain.
Problem list: Musculoskeletal low back strain; Diabetes; [Unknown]
Plan:
I ordered a low back x-ray to evaluate the joint spaces and possible disk issues.
I also ordered physical therapy to help with pain and strengthen the muscles in the back.
I prescribed 400mg of ibuprofen to be taken occasionally with Tylenol for severe pain, up to 3 times a day, and one Flexeril per day in the evening.
I instructed the patient to continue with their current diet and exercise routine and to continue taking metformin.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the x-ray and A1c test.
The patient will also be referred to a pain doctor if the pain persists after the x-ray and physical therapy.
